topic: Investigate whether .opencode/plugins/mk-deep-loop-guard.js can be extended to mechanically detect and optionally block repeated/loop-like orchestrate-to-command-owned-loop-executor dispatches while allowing exactly one legitimate bounded hand-off.

focus: SDK session-state capabilities + mechanical loop-like definition

answeredQuestions: Q1: SDK exposes sessionID; in-process Map and external file both proven
Q2: same loop-executor N>=3 per session = block; prompt-parsed identity

focus: Dispatch path grounding + phase 012 evidence + 2 concrete design options
noveltyJustification: New evidence: 4-document contract chain for bounded hand-off, iteration-state heuristic for caller attribution, and 2 concrete design options with trade-offs. Partially builds on iter 1's SDK/state findings.
answeredQuestions: Q3: 'exactly one bounded hand-off' stated in 4 docs; guard cannot attribute caller from args
Q4: GPT-5.5 inconsistently enforced Command-only (refused research, allowed review)
Design: Option A (in-process) vs Option B (external + iteration-aware) with trade-offs

ruledOut.reason: Would false-positive on legitimate command-driven loop iterations
ruledOut.evidence: loop_protocol.md dispatches @deep-research N times per loop
